license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.buggnttw
title: Collection and  Post-Surgical Excision of Human Kidney Tissue through the Cooperative Human Tissue Network
author: Maya Brewer

steps_raw.instruction: Kidney tissues are selected based on patient and clinical metadata (e.g. age<65 years old). All patients have consented to allow their tissue to be used for research purposes as part of this study.
Receive excised kidney from full nephrectomy.
Cut kidney in half along the longest plane and open like a book.
Cut out a portion of the kidney (~80 x 60 x 4 mm) that is farthest away from the tumor location.
Take a picture of the smaller piece of kidney in reference to its original location within the entire kidney if possible.
Cut the piece of kidney into ~4 smaller, rectangular pieces (~2 x 15 x 4 mm).
Image each of these smaller pieces in reference to their original position.
Place tissue in plastic container on ice. Transfer tissue to those preparing tissue for long-term storage.
